title: 10 best herbal capsules for hemorrhoids

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain a soothing and anti-inflammatory compound called hamamelan, which is extracted from the bark of the witch hazel tree. When taken orally in capsule form, this natural ingredient can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, promoting healing and relief from symptoms such as itching, bleeding, and pain. Additionally, witch hazel has astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, further alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch hazel's astringent properties help to constrict and reduce the swelling of hemorrhoid tissue, providing relief from pain, itching, and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in witch hazel capsules, such as gallic acid and tannins, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Antiseptic properties: Witch hazel's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to hemorrhoidal discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: These astringent compounds help reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, which can alleviate swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the irritated tissues surrounding hemorrhoids.

Hamamelitannin: This unique tannin derivative is responsible for the astringent and anti-inflammatory effects of witch hazel, making it effective in reducing discomfort, itching, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioflavonoids and phenolic acids, making them effective for treating hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Leaves: Contain high amounts of tannins, which help to reduce inflammation, itching, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: Have been traditionally used to treat hemorrhoids due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried hamamelis virginiana flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 1 part of gelatin in a bowl to create a mixture.
Add a small amount of water to the mixture and stir until the gelatin dissolves completely in 10 minutes.
Fill 250 capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain aescin, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the anal veins. This can help alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, horse chestnut's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to shrink the size of hemorrhoids, providing relief from painful bowel movements and reducing the risk of bleeding.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a key component of horse chestnut extract, helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones present in horse chestnut extract have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to relieve pain, itching, and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in horse chestnut extract exhibit antioxidant activity, which helps to protect the mucous membranes and blood vessels from oxidative damage, reducing the risk of hemorrhoidal bleeding and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Aescin: Aescin is a triterpenoid saponin that helps with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and strengthening blood vessel walls, which can help alleviate swelling and pain.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Aesculus hippocastanum capsules have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation, improve blood flow, and protect tissues from oxidative damage, which can contribute to hemorrhoid relief.

Saponins (Esculin and Esculin): Saponins in Aesculus hippocastanum capsules have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and improve wound healing, which can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their aescin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: The barks are used because of their aescin content and other bioactive compounds that aid in soothing and shrinking hemorrhoidal tissues.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their aescin content and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of hemorrhoidal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of aesculus hippocastanum roots and stems to use in the herbal capsule preparation.
Dry the prepared aesculus hippocastanum material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 50 grams of the aesculus hippocastanum powder with 250 grams of vegetable capsules in a clean environment.
Fill 250 vegetable capsules with the aesculus hippocastanum powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of vaccinium macrocarpon fruits at peak ripeness and dry them in a single layer for 2 days at room temperature.
Grind the dried vaccinium macrocarpon into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 2g of the powder with 1g of gelatin in a small bowl to prepare the capsule filling.
Fill 100 capsules using a capsule filler machine with the prepared vaccinium macrocarpon mixture in a batch time of 30 minutes.
Store the herbal vaccinium macrocarpon capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in early morning with a clean pair of scissors for quality.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 50 grams of dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 milliliters of gelatin with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Fill the gelatin mixture into empty vegetarian capsules and add 250 milligrams of the tilia platyphyllos powder to each capsule.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 500g of gelatin capsules in a clean environment.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mill or blender for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 90g of microcrystalline cellulose and 0.1g of magnesium stearate.
Fill the gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand at a rate of 10 capsules per minute.
Label the capsules with the name ginkgo biloba 100mg and the recommended dosage of 1-2 capsules per day.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried solanum dulcamara flowers and leaves from a clean and well-maintained garden.

Mix the 20g of fine powder with 40g of gelatin and 20g of water in a bowl.
Use a dropper to fill 20 capsules with the solanum dulcamara mixture and seal them using a hand-held capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain a concentrated form of aloe vera's active compound, aloin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The capsules help to reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids, while also promoting healing and tissue repair. Additionally, the aloe vera in the capsules can help to improve digestion and reduce constipation, common contributing factors to hemorrhoids. This natural remedy provides effective relief from hemorrhoid symptoms without harsh chemicals or invasive procedures.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory agents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and cinnamic acid, which can reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Stool softening agents: Aloe vera's mucilages and polysaccharides help to soften stool, making it easier to pass through the anus and reducing straining, which is a common cause of hemorrhoid irritation.

Wound-healing and tissue repair compounds: Aloe vera contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote wound healing, which can aid in repairing damaged tissues around the anal area and reduce bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote healing of hemorrhoidal tissue by exerting anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the rectal area.
Polysaccharides: The gel-like properties of polysaccharides help soothe and protect the rectal mucosa, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation of hemorrhoidal tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their high content of aloe-emodin and other anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh aloe vera gel from 3 to 4 aloe vera plants and clean it thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 to 48 hours.
Weigh 20 grams of the dried aloe vera gel and mix it with 80 grams of gelatin powder.
Add distilled water to the gelatin mixture and mix it well until the gelatin is fully dissolved.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 empty capsules with the aloe vera gel mixture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and roots in a sterile environment to prevent contamination.
Dry the harvested symphytum officinale leaves and roots in a temperature-controlled area at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried symphytum officinale leaves and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the symphytum officinale powder and seal them immediately.

Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers at peak bloom in the morning after dew has evaporated, collecting 100g for use.

Grind the dried gardenia jasminoides flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, achieving a uniform consistency with 10 minutes of grinding.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of paeonia suffruticosa in the morning after dew has evaporated for freshness.
Grind 30-40 grams of dried paeonia suffruticosa roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 15 grams of powdered paeonia suffruticosa with 15 grams of excipient material such as microcrystalline cellulose in a stainless steel bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency taking approximately 2 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature protected from light and moisture for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with hemorrhoids because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the affected area, reducing swelling and discomfort. The herb's flavonoids also help to strengthen blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing pressure on the veins, which can contribute to hemorrhoid formation. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects can ease bowel movements, reducing strain and discomfort associated with constipation, a common cause of hemorrhoids. As a natural remedy, chamomile capsules offer a gentle and effective way to alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compound, apigenin, reduces swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the rectal tissue from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hemorrhoid formation and exacerbate symptoms.
Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's natural sedative properties help relax the anal sphincter muscles, reducing itching, burning, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm inflamed tissues associated with hemorrhoids.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own according to local regulations.
Grind 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 100g of gelatin and 10ml of distilled water in a clean bowl to form a uniform paste for 2 hours.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the matricaria chamomilla-gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine or a spoon for 1 hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 12 months to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain potent natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to shrink swollen veins and tissues, providing relief from itching, burning, and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, the capsules may also help to improve digestion, reduce straining during bowel movements, and promote regularity, all of which can contribute to a reduction in hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Wild potato contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.

Vasoprotective effects: The vasoprotective properties of wild potato help to strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation, which can reduce the risk of bleeding and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.

Prolonged healing and tissue repair: Wild potato contains compounds that promote wound healing and tissue repair, helping to speed up the recovery process and reduce the severity of hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in starch and fiber, rhyzomes help to soothe and reduce inflammation associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: With their high antioxidant content, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum kok saghyz roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated, weighing approximately 500 grams.
Cleanse the harvested roots by rinsing them with cold running water and gently scrubbing away any dirt or debris, taking about 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots using a food dehydrator set at low temperature for 6 hours, or alternatively, air-drying them for 24 hours in a warm place.
Grind the dried taraxacum kok saghyz roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, taking about 5 minutes to achieve desired consistency.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powdered taraxacum kok saghyz root and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months, or refrigerate for up to 1 month.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Quaking aspen capsules helps with hemorrhoids because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation, soothe discomfort, and promote healing. The herb contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that targets swollen veins and tissues, providing relief from itching, burning, and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, quaking aspen's antioxidant properties help to repair damaged tissue and promote collagen synthesis, leading to faster recovery and improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Quaking aspen's compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Quaking aspen's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hemorrhoid development and worsening symptoms.

Astringent and anti-hemorrhagic effects: Quaking aspen's astringent compounds help reduce bleeding and shrink swollen tissues, while its anti-hemorrhagic properties may aid in stopping or reducing bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the quaking aspen plant are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the anal area.
Rhyzomes: Quaking aspen rhyzomes contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing hemorrhoidal pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh populus tremuloides leaves in late summer or early fall weighing about 500 grams.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 6 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle weighing about 100 grams.
Mix the powdered populus tremuloides with gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:10 and fill each capsule with approximately 500 milligrams.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer preservation.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hemorrhoids are natural supplements containing extracts from various plants that help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids, such as itching, bleeding, and pain. These capsules work by soothing inflamed tissues, reducing swelling, and promoting healing. The best herbal capsules for hemorrhoids include Aloe barbadensis, Hamamelis virginiana, Vaccinium myrtillus, Aesculus hippocastanum, Psidium guajava, Plantago lanceolata, Plantago ovata, Ginkgo biloba, Cinchona officinalis, and Ruscus aculeatus. If left untreated, hemorrhoids can lead to severe discomfort, pain, and even social isolation, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hemorrhoids that are treated with herbal capsules are anal fissures, piles, and proctitis. These conditions cause discomfort, pain, and bleeding in the anal area, often due to inflammation or irritation. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like psyllium, aloe vera, and witch hazel can help soothe and calm these symptoms, reducing swelling and promoting healing. Some herbal remedies also aim to address underlying causes such as constipation or digestive issues that may contribute to these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with hemorrhoids is a blend of Witch Hazel, Psyllium, and Aloe Vera. Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, while Psyllium's fiber content helps to soften stool and ease bowel movements. Aloe Vera's soothing properties calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. This combination works synergistically to alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids, including itching, bleeding, and pain. Consult a healthcare professional before adding any supplements to your regimen.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain the soothing and anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera gel. The gel is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the rectal area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, aloe vera capsules can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids. Regular use of aloe vera capsules can also help to reduce the frequency and severity of hemorrhoid episodes, providing long-term relief for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Polysaccharides: Assist in soothing and protecting the mucous membrane of the anal region, promoting healing and reducing irritation.
Glycoproteins: Contribute to the anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, helping to calm the discomfort and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a gel-like substance with anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing hemorrhoid symptoms.
Roots: They are used to extract a compound called aloin, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: They are also a source of aloe-emodin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, useful in treating hemorrhoid discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Dry the aloe barbadensis leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to prevent nutrient loss.
Grind the dried aloe barbadensis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered aloe barbadensis with a binding agent like gelatin at a 1:1 ratio to facilitate capsule formation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe barbadensis mixture using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain the extract of the witch hazel plant, which has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The active compounds in witch hazel, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to reduce swelling and itching in the rectal area. The astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the affected area and promoting faster healing. Additionally, witch hazel has natural antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for healing.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids by constricting blood vessels and preventing blood from entering the affected area.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Phenolic acids: Exhibit astringent properties, helping to reduce bleeding and alleviate pain by constricting blood vessels and promoting healing in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their astringent properties that help reduce swelling and bleeding.
Barks: The barks of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help soothe and protect the affected area.
Leaves: The leaves of Hamamelis virginiana are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Weigh 100g of the dried plant material and mix it with 100g of gelatin capsule powder in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine at 150 capsules per hour.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or use within 12 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas. These capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, Vaccinium myrtillus is rich in fiber, which helps to soften stool and prevent constipation, a common cause of hemorrhoids. By addressing the root causes of hemorrhoids, Vaccinium myrtillus capsules can provide relief and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids by counteracting free radicals that contribute to tissue damage.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, quercetin helps reduce swelling and shrink the size of hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Tannins: These astringent compounds help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding associated with hemorrhoids by promoting the formation of a protective barrier on the surface of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Fruits: Utilized because they contain anthocyanins and other antioxidants that can reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: Employed for their astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and itching caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of vaccinium myrtillus berries that have been dried for at least 24 hours in a cool dark place.
Crush the dried berries using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils and nutrients for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the crushed berries with 2 tablespoons of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of glycerin and 1 tablespoon of water to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain horse chestnut extract, which is rich in aescin, a powerful anti-inflammatory agent. Aescin helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the veins, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. By improving blood flow and reducing fluid retention, Aesculus hippocastanum capsules can also help to shrink hemorrhoid tissue and promote healing. Regular use of these capsules may provide relief from hemorrhoid symptoms and promote overall venous health.
remedy_constituents: Aescin: Aescin is a saponin triterpene that helps reduce hemorrhoid inflammation and swelling by improving blood vessel permeability and reducing fluid leakage into the interstitial tissue.

Aesculetin: Aesculetin is a coumarin derivative that helps alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thus reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair.

Alkaloids (specifically Aesculin): Alkaloids in Aesculus hippocastanum help with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation, improving blood circulation, and exerting a protective effect on blood vessels, which in turn alleviates the discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe and calm the rectal area.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids because of their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissue.
Barks: They are used to make capsules for hemorrhoids due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce pain, itching, and inflammation in the rectal area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature aesculus hippocastanum seeds in late summer when they turn brown and dry completely.
Dry the harvested seeds in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 12 hours.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or high-speed grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered seed with 500 milligrams of gelatin powder and a small amount of water.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Psidium guajava capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the irritated tissue in the affected area. The capsules also possess astringent properties that help reduce bleeding and promote the healing process. Additionally, the natural compounds present in Psidium guajava capsules can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, providing quick relief and promoting overall digestive health. This natural remedy is often used to treat hemorrhoids due to its effectiveness.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These constituents help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids such as swelling and pain.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that can help reduce bleeding and soothe irritated tissues, promoting healing and reducing discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain, while also promoting the formation of new tissue to repair damaged areas in the rectal region.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Fruits: Utilized because of their anthraquinone content, which has laxative properties that help in treating constipation, a common cause of hemorrhoids.
Barks: Employed for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing bleeding and pain from hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh psidium guajava leaves and dry them in a warm place for 3 days.
Grind 200 grams of the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer for 5 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain mucilage, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated tissues. This soothing effect reduces swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from painful symptoms. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. The anti-inflammatory properties also aid in promoting healing and reducing inflammation, making it an effective herbal remedy to alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms and promote overall comfort.
remedy_constituents: Apolysaccharides: They help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery of hemorrhoidal tissues.
Plantaginin: It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the affected area, reducing the severity of hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and itching associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried plantago lanceolata leaves and flowers at the peak of potency and freshness.
Crush the dried material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 5 grams of a suitable capsule filler and add a small amount of gelatin.
Use a capsule machine to fill the capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago ovata capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they are derived from the seeds of the plantago ovata plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The seeds contain a gel-forming fiber called mucilage, which forms a protective barrier around the affected area, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. This natural barrier also helps to promote healing and reduce bleeding, making plantago ovata capsules a popular natural remedy for hemorrhoid relief.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: Mucilage helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the rectal area, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the hemorrhoid-affected area, promoting healing and relief.
Apolypodine: Apolypodine has astringent properties that help reduce bleeding and inflammation in hemorrhoids, promoting faster healing and alleviation of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used because they are rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the rectal area.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to relieve hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried plantago ovata seeds and 20g of gelatin powder.
Grind the plantago ovata seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground plantago ovata powder with gelatin powder and 100ml of hot water.
Pour the mixture into a capsule machine and fill 100 capsules with the mixture.
Dry the filled capsules in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing swelling and inflammation. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties in ginkgo biloba help to soothe and calm the irritated tissues. This leads to a reduction in pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's ability to thin the blood and prevent clotting can help to ease the passage of stool and reduce the risk of hemorrhoids occurring in the first place, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling in hemorrhoids by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.
Quercetin: Acts as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in hemorrhoidal tissues, which helps alleviate symptoms such as pain and itching.

Ginkgolides: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-exudative properties, which help reduce bleeding and inflammation in hemorrhoids by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF) and other inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Ginkgo biloba, leaves are used due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which helps to alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are used due to their ability to improve blood circulation, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves (again): Leaves are also used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin and tissues affected by hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Grind 200 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Heat 200 milliliters of distilled water to 60-70 degrees celsius in a saucepan for 5 minutes.
Add the gelatin mixture to the warm water and stir for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain quinine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. The quinine in Cinchona officinalis capsules helps to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis capsules have been traditionally used to improve blood circulation and promote the healing of damaged tissues, which can aid in the recovery from hemorrhoid symptoms. This natural remedy has been used to provide relief from itching, discomfort, and rectal bleeding caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_constituents: Quinovic glycosides: These compounds help with hemorrhoids by reducing inflammation and alleviating pain associated with hemorrhoid swelling.
Alkaloids (specifically Quinine): Quinine helps with hemorrhoids by reducing blood vessel constriction and improving blood flow to the affected area, thus promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
Tannins: Tannins help with hemorrhoids by contracting blood vessels and reducing swelling, which in turn alleviates pain and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain quinine and other alkaloids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain flavonoids, which help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in hemorrhoids.
Roots: The roots of Cinchona officinalis contain glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in soothing and healing hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 300g of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a reputable source and store it in a cool dry place.
Grind 100g of the cinchona bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground cinchona powder with 100g of tapioca starch to prevent clumping for 2 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the cinchona-tapioca mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus capsules helps with hemorrhoids because they contain compounds that improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties of Ruscus aculeatus help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, it aids in the reduction of fluid retention, which can contribute to hemorrhoid formation. By alleviating symptoms and promoting healing, Ruscus aculeatus capsules provide a natural and effective solution for individuals experiencing hemorrhoid discomfort. Regular use can lead to improved overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with hemorrhoids, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Phenolic acids: They possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe and protect the affected area, promoting healing and reducing symptoms.
Flavonoids: These compounds may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can aid in the reduction of swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote healing.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to alleviate hemorrhoid symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used due to their astringent properties, which can help to reduce swelling and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Dry the ruscus aculeatus leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a small bowl for 30 seconds.
Fill a capsule maker with the gelatin mixture and compress the mixture using the machine.
Store the herbal ruscus aculeatus capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
